title: organization — driver + strategy extension points
description: Organization has no third-party driver plug-ins. It exposes ONE internal strategy contract — `CycleDetectorStrategyInterface` — as an extension seam so future modules (for instance a policy-as-code overlay) can substitute a different tree-cycle algorithm without forking the module. This file documents that contract for completeness; the framework ships one implementation.

cycle_detector.purpose: Given a proposed (child_id, new_parent_id) tuple, decide whether the change would introduce a cycle in the tenant's Organization tree. Runs synchronously in the write path.

methods.returns: CycleDetectionResult DTO: would_cycle (bool), cycle_path (array<string> of ids forming the cycle when detected), diagnostics (array).

tree_path_builder.purpose: Given a node + its resolved parent chain, produce the materialised `.a.b.c.` path string. Extension seam only — the framework implementation is optimal for the sentinel-wrapped format.

methods.description: Split a stored tree_path back into an ordered array of ids (root → self).

framework_strategies.name: AncestorWalkCycleDetector
framework_strategies.purpose: Default implementation. Walks the proposed parent's ancestor chain (via existing tree_path — no recursive query needed) looking for the child id. O(depth) per call; typical depth ≤ 5 so effectively constant.

framework_strategies.name: DottedTreePathBuilder
framework_strategies.purpose: Default `.a.b.c.` sentinel-wrapped builder. Uses config('organization.hierarchy.tree_path_separator') as the join char.

extension_note: New strategies register via the container: `$container->bind(CycleDetectorStrategyInterface::class, MyStrategy::class)`. The observer + the reparent action resolve through the interface, so no call site changes when swapping. No tenant-configurable override — this is set-once at boot.
